warnings.message: Outcome triggeredBy conditions are not mutually exclusive and no resolution priority/logic is specified. A single playthrough can satisfy conditions for more than one outcome simultaneously (e.g., choice-5:a alone satisfies part of 'branch-reckoning' while choice-2:a + choice-3:a + choice-6:a fully satisfies 'branch-authentic' — if the engine treats triggeredBy as ANY-match rather than ALL-match, both could fire). The episode never specifies whether triggeredBy entries are AND'd or OR'd, nor a tiebreak/priority order.
warnings.suggestion: Document (or enforce upstream) whether triggeredBy is evaluated as full-set match (all listed choice:option pairs must be present) or any-match, and define a priority order for resolving overlapping matches — e.g., most-specific (most matched conditions) wins, with 'branch-reckoning' or 'branch-authentic' as explicit tiebreak winner.

warnings.message: choice-4 (the presentation-day 'true story vs. safe reel' choice) is not referenced by any outcome's triggeredBy list, despite being a major, emotionally central decision point (own dedicated scenes 9a/9b) and having a notable trait swing (CONFIDENCE +2 vs -1). Its result currently has no bearing on which ending branch/branchDialogue variant fires.
warnings.suggestion: Either fold choice-4 outcomes into the triggeredBy conditions for the three branches, or explicitly note that choice-4 affects trait totals only and is intentionally excluded from branch selection.

warnings.message: choice-1:b and choice-5:b are never referenced in any outcome's triggeredBy list, meaning some individual choice outcomes contribute to trait totals but never factor into which ending narrative variant is shown.
warnings.suggestion: Confirm this is intentional (only a subset of choices gate the ending flavor text) or add coverage for completeness/symmetry.

warnings.message: Minor tonal/logic note: in choice-1, option 'b' ('Brush it off — partners are partners') is the choice that avoids helping Maya undermine the pairing, yet it maps to JUDGMENT:+2/CONFIDENCE:+1, while option 'a' (going to Alvarez, which Wren does with visible ambivalence, not malice) maps to JUDGMENT:-1. This is a defensible authorial call but worth a sanity check since 'informing a teacher of a pairing concern' is a much milder infraction than the scene text implies it should be judged as.
warnings.suggestion: No change required; flagging for narrative-design review only.

recommendations: Add an explicit spec note (in the outcomes schema or a comment) clarifying how triggeredBy arrays are evaluated (ANY vs ALL) and how conflicts between overlapping outcomes are resolved at runtime.
Consider incorporating choice-4's result into at least one outcome's triggeredBy list so the presentation-day choice has narrative as well as trait-based consequences.

Full choice/scene graph was traced end-to-end (scene-1 → ... → scene-12 → END across all three choice-6 branches) with no unreachable scenes, no dangling nextScene/defaultNextScene references, no duplicate scene/choice/dialogue ids, and no character references outside the defined roster — this portion of the episode is solid and ready to ship as-is.
